items.title: USDA lists SNAP food-restriction waivers as states prepare to limit purchases like soda and candy
items.summary: USDA FNS posted an updated list of approved SNAP food-restriction waivers that allow states to restrict purchases of items like soda and candy. The list shows upcoming target implementation dates including Florida (04/20/26: soda, energy drinks, candy, prepared desserts), Colorado (04/30/26: soft drinks), and Arkansas (07/01/26: soda, <50% juice drinks, unhealthy drinks, candy), among others.

items.title: CMS guidance: Medicaid expansion adults will move to 6-month eligibility renewals starting Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS issued guidance implementing a federal requirement for most Medicaid expansion adults (section 1902(a)(10)(A)(i)(VIII)) to have eligibility redeterminations every 6 months (instead of 12) for renewals scheduled on or after 2027-01-01. CMS says states can either reschedule renewal initiation dates to earlier in 2027 or transition people at their next scheduled 2027 renewal, and states must submit a state plan amendment via MACPro by 2027-03-31 to attest to compliance.

items.title: HUD changes Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher rules: PHAs must follow standard SSN and immigration-status verification regs
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes prior flexibility that allowed some Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher admissions without initial SSN or eligible immigration documentation, with families providing documents within 180 days. HUD says PHAs must follow 24 CFR 5.508 (citizenship/eligible immigration status) and 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 (SSN disclosure/verification) and must be in compliance within 60 days of the notice’s publication.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD rescinds multiple COVID-era notices affecting Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) and public housing programs
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-06 rescinds a wide list of CARES Act and COVID-19-related PIH notices and partially rescinds earlier COVID waiver guidance for Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) and Public Housing programs. HUD also revises PIH 2020-32 by striking certain language-access instructions for remote hearings/briefings, while noting PHAs still must comply with applicable federal language-access laws.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; early EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2
items.summary: The IRS opened the 2026 filing season on 2026-01-26 and said most Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) refunds will be available by 2026-03-02 for taxpayers using direct deposit with no other issues. The IRS also said Where’s My Refund? would show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by 2026-02-21.

items.title: USDA: FY2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts set; states must offer at least one non-fresh option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS set FY2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher/benefit amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or partially breastfeeding participants. USDA also notes WIC state agencies must authorize at least one non-fresh form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: Education Department proposes rules for new Workforce Pell; program would start July 2026 for short-term programs as short as 8 weeks
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education issued proposed rules to implement Workforce Pell Grants, allowing Pell use for eligible short-term programs with 150–599 clock hours that last at least 8 weeks but less than 15 weeks. The proposal says eligible programs must be approved by a Governor (after consultation with the state workforce board) and meet accountability benchmarks like completion and job placement rates; public comments are due by 2026-04-08.

items.title: SSA: 2026 COLA is 2.8%; SSI max federal benefit is $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (couple)
items.summary: SSA says Social Security and SSI recipients receive a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026. SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists the SSI federal payment standard at $994/month for an individual and $1,491/month for a couple, and lists disability work thresholds including SGA at $1,690/month (non-blind) and a Trial Work Period amount of $1,210/month.

items.title: SSA publishes 2026–2027 payment schedule (SSI paid on the 1st; Social Security on the 3rd for some recipients)
items.summary: SSA published its 2026–2027 payment calendar and notes that people who received Social Security before May 1997, or who receive both Social Security and SSI, typically get Social Security on the 3rd and SSI on the 1st. The calendar also lists the Wednesday payment schedule by birthdate range and reminds recipients to allow three additional mailing days before contacting SSA if a payment is late.

items.title: USDA opens comment period on WIC Breastfeeding Award of Excellence information collection (comments due May 11, 2026)
items.summary: USDA FNS published a Federal Register notice seeking public comment on a proposed information collection for the WIC Breastfeeding Award of Excellence program. The notice says written comments must be received by 2026-05-11 and explains the collection supports recognizing local WIC agencies with exemplary breastfeeding services and support.
items.source: USDA Food and Nutrition Service / Federal Register

items.title: Federal Student Aid: maximum Pell Grant for the 2026–27 award year is $7,395
items.summary: Federal Student Aid notes that the maximum Federal Pell Grant award for the 2026–27 award year is $7,395. The guidance also reminds students they may be able to receive up to 150% of their yearly Pell Grant award in the same year if they attend an additional term (often called year-round Pell).
